Fleet review of PR #531: "build(deps): bump actions/upload-artifact from 4 to 7" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/upload-artifact-7 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/upload-artifact](https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact) from 4 to 7.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754
  • Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#762
  • Support direct file uploads by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#764

New Contributors

  • @​Link- made their first contribution in

Fleet review of PR #534: "build(deps-dev): bump playwright from 1.60.0 to 1.61.0" Branch: dependabot/npm_and_yarn/playwright-1.61.0 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, javascript ## PR Description Bumps [playwright](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright) from 1.60.0 to 1.61.0.
Release notes

Sourced from playwright's releases.

v1.61.0

🔑 WebAuthn passkeys

New Credentials virtual authenticator, available via browserContext.credentials, lets tests register passkeys and answer navigator.credentials.create() / navigator.credentials.get() ceremonies in the page — no real hardware key required, works in all browsers:

const context = await browser.newContext();

// Seed a passkey your backend provisioned for a test user.

await context.credentials.create('example.com', {

id: credentialId,

userHandle,

privateKey,

publicKey,

});

await context.credentials.install();


const page = await context.newPage();

await page.goto('https://example.com/login');

// The page's navigator.credentials.get() is answered with the seeded passkey.

You can also let the app register a passkey once in a setup test, read it back with credentials.get(), and seed it into later tests — see Credentials for details.

🗃️ Web Storage

New WebStorage API, available via

Fleet review of PR #529: "build(deps): bump actions/github-script from 7 to 9" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/github-script-9 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/github-script](https://github.com/actions/github-script) from 7 to 9.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/github-script's releases.

v9.0.0

New features:

  • getOctokit factory function — Available directly in the script context. Create additional authenticated Octokit clients with different tokens for multi-token workflows, GitHub App tokens, and cross-org access. See Creating additional clients with getOctokit for details and examples.
  • Orchestration ID in user-agent — The ACTIONS_ORCHESTRATION_ID environment variable is automatically appended to the user-agent string for request tracing.

Breaking changes:

  • require('@actions/github') no longer works in scripts. The upgrade to @actions/github v9 (ESM-only) means require('@actions/github') will fail at runtime. If you previously used patterns like const { getOctokit } = require('@actions/github') to create secondary clients, use the new injected getOctokit function instead — it's available directly in the script context with no imports needed.
  • getOctokit is now an injected function parameter. Scripts that declare const getOctokit = ... or let getOctokit = ... will get a SyntaxError because JavaScript does not allow const/let redeclaratio

Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754
  • Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#762
  • Support direct file uploads by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#764

Fleet review of PR #537: "chore(deps-dev): bump playwright from 1.60.0 to 1.61.0 in /apps/lantern-garage" Branch: dependabot/npm_and_yarn/apps/lantern-garage/playwright-1.61.0 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, javascript ## PR Description Bumps [playwright](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright) from 1.60.0 to 1.61.0.
Release notes

Sourced from playwright's releases.

v1.61.0

🔑 WebAuthn passkeys

New Credentials virtual authenticator, available via browserContext.credentials, lets tests register passkeys and answer navigator.credentials.create() / navigator.credentials.get() ceremonies in the page — no real hardware key required, works in all browsers:

const context = await browser.newContext();

// Seed a passkey your backend provisioned for a test user.

await context.credentials.create('example.com', {

id: credentialId,

userHandle,

privateKey,

publicKey,

});

await context.credentials.install();


const page = await context.newPage();

await page.goto('https://example.com/login');

// The page's navigator.credentials.get() is answered with the seeded passkey.

You can also let the app register a passkey once in a setup test, read it back with credentials.get(), and seed it into later tests — see Credentials for details.

🗃️ Web Storage

New WebStorage A

Fleet review of PR #529: "build(deps): bump actions/github-script from 7 to 9" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/github-script-9 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/github-script](https://github.com/actions/github-script) from 7 to 9.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/github-script's releases.

v9.0.0

New features:

  • getOctokit factory function — Available directly in the script context. Create additional authenticated Octokit clients with different tokens for multi-token workflows, GitHub App tokens, and cross-org access. See Creating additional clients with getOctokit for details and examples.
  • Orchestration ID in user-agent — The ACTIONS_ORCHESTRATION_ID environment variable is automatically appended to the user-agent string for request tracing.

Breaking changes:

  • require('@actions/github') no longer works in scripts. The upgrade to @actions/github v9 (ESM-only) means require('@actions/github') will fail at runtime. If you previously used patterns like const { getOctokit } = require('@actions/github') to create secondary clients, use the new injected getOctokit function instead — it's available directly in the script context with no imports needed.
  • getOctokit is now an injected function parameter. Scripts that declare const getOctokit = ... or let getOctokit = ... will get a SyntaxError because JavaScript does not allow const/let redeclaratio

Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754
  • Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#762
  • Support direct file uploads by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#764

Release notes

Sourced from playwright's releases.

v1.61.0

🔑 WebAuthn passkeys

New Credentials virtual authenticator, available via browserContext.credentials, lets tests register passkeys and answer navigator.credentials.create() / navigator.credentials.get() ceremonies in the page — no real hardware key required, works in all browsers:

const context = await browser.newContext();

// Seed a passkey your backend provisioned for a test user.

await context.credentials.create('example.com', {

id: credentialId,

userHandle,

privateKey,

publicKey,

});

await context.credentials.install();


const page = await context.newPage();

await page.goto('https://example.com/login');

// The page's navigator.credentials.get() is answered with the seeded passkey.

You can also let the app register a passkey once in a setup test, read it back with credentials.get(), and seed it into later tests — see Credentials for details.

🗃️ Web Storage

New WebStorage API, available via

Fleet review of PR #529: "build(deps): bump actions/github-script from 7 to 9" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/github-script-9 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/github-script](https://github.com/actions/github-script) from 7 to 9.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/github-script's releases.

v9.0.0

New features:

  • getOctokit factory function — Available directly in the script context. Create additional authenticated Octokit clients with different tokens for multi-token workflows, GitHub App tokens, and cross-org access. See Creating additional clients with getOctokit for details and examples.
  • Orchestration ID in user-agent — The ACTIONS_ORCHESTRATION_ID environment variable is automatically appended to the user-agent string for request tracing.

Breaking changes:

  • require('@actions/github') no longer works in scripts. The upgrade to @actions/github v9 (ESM-only) means require('@actions/github') will fail at runtime. If you previously used patterns like const { getOctokit } = require('@actions/github') to create secondary clients, use the new injected getOctokit function instead — it's available directly in the script context with no imports needed.
  • getOctokit is now an injected function parameter. Scripts that declare const getOctokit = ... or let getOctokit = ... will get a SyntaxError because JavaScript does not allow const/let redeclaratio

Fleet review of PR #531: "build(deps): bump actions/upload-artifact from 4 to 7" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/upload-artifact-7 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/upload-artifact](https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact) from 4 to 7.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754
  • Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#762
  • Support direct file uploads by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#764

New Contributors

  • @​Link- made their first contribution in

Fleet review of PR #534: "build(deps-dev): bump playwright from 1.60.0 to 1.61.0" Branch: dependabot/npm_and_yarn/playwright-1.61.0 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, javascript ## PR Description Bumps [playwright](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright) from 1.60.0 to 1.61.0.
Release notes

Sourced from playwright's releases.

v1.61.0

🔑 WebAuthn passkeys

New Credentials virtual authenticator, available via browserContext.credentials, lets tests register passkeys and answer navigator.credentials.create() / navigator.credentials.get() ceremonies in the page — no real hardware key required, works in all browsers:

const context = await browser.newContext();

// Seed a passkey your backend provisioned for a test user.

await context.credentials.create('example.com', {

id: credentialId,

userHandle,

privateKey,

publicKey,

});

await context.credentials.install();


const page = await context.newPage();

await page.goto('https://example.com/login');

// The page's navigator.credentials.get() is answered with the seeded passkey.

You can also let the app register a passkey once in a setup test, read it back with credentials.get(), and seed it into later tests — see Credentials for details.

🗃️ Web Storage

New WebStorage API, available via

Fleet review of PR #529: "build(deps): bump actions/github-script from 7 to 9" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/github-script-9 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/github-script](https://github.com/actions/github-script) from 7 to 9.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/github-script's releases.

v9.0.0

New features:

  • getOctokit factory function — Available directly in the script context. Create additional authenticated Octokit clients with different tokens for multi-token workflows, GitHub App tokens, and cross-org access. See Creating additional clients with getOctokit for details and examples.
  • Orchestration ID in user-agent — The ACTIONS_ORCHESTRATION_ID environment variable is automatically appended to the user-agent string for request tracing.

Breaking changes:

  • require('@actions/github') no longer works in scripts. The upgrade to @actions/github v9 (ESM-only) means require('@actions/github') will fail at runtime. If you previously used patterns like const { getOctokit } = require('@actions/github') to create secondary clients, use the new injected getOctokit function instead — it's available directly in the script context with no imports needed.
  • getOctokit is now an injected function parameter. Scripts that declare const getOctokit = ... or let getOctokit = ... will get a SyntaxError because JavaScript does not allow const/let redeclaratio

Release notes

Sourced from @​playwright/test's releases.

v1.61.0

🔑 WebAuthn passkeys

New Credentials virtual authenticator, available via browserContext.credentials, lets tests register passkeys and answer navigator.credentials.create() / navigator.credentials.get() ceremonies in the page — no real hardware key required, works in all browsers:

const context = await browser.newContext();

// Seed a passkey your backend provisioned for a test user.

await context.credentials.create('example.com', {

id: credentialId,

userHandle,

privateKey,

publicKey,

});

await context.credentials.install();


const page = await context.newPage();

await page.goto('https://example.com/login');

// The page's navigator.credentials.get() is answered with the seeded passkey.

You can also let the app register a passkey once in a setup test, read it back with credentials.get(), and seed it into later tests — see Credentials for details.

New WebStorage API, available via

Fleet review of PR #531: "build(deps): bump actions/upload-artifact from 4 to 7" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/upload-artifact-7 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/upload-artifact](https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact) from 4 to 7.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754
  • Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#762
  • Support direct file uploads by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#764

New Contributors

  • @​Link- made their first contribution in

Fleet review of PR #529: "build(deps): bump actions/github-script from 7 to 9" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/github-script-9 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/github-script](https://github.com/actions/github-script) from 7 to 9.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/github-script's releases.

v9.0.0

New features:

  • getOctokit factory function — Available directly in the script context. Create additional authenticated Octokit clients with different tokens for multi-token workflows, GitHub App tokens, and cross-org access. See Creating additional clients with getOctokit for details and examples.
  • Orchestration ID in user-agent — The ACTIONS_ORCHESTRATION_ID environment variable is automatically appended to the user-agent string for request tracing.

Breaking changes:

  • require('@actions/github') no longer works in scripts. The upgrade to @actions/github v9 (ESM-only) means require('@actions/github') will fail at runtime. If you previously used patterns like const { getOctokit } = require('@actions/github') to create secondary clients, use the new injected getOctokit function instead — it's available directly in the script context with no imports needed.
  • getOctokit is now an injected function parameter. Scripts that declare const getOctokit = ... or let getOctokit = ... will get a SyntaxError because JavaScript does not allow const/let redeclaratio

Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754
  • Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#762
  • Support direct file uploads by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#764

Fleet review of PR #529: "build(deps): bump actions/github-script from 7 to 9" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/github-script-9 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/github-script](https://github.com/actions/github-script) from 7 to 9.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/github-script's releases.

v9.0.0

New features:

  • getOctokit factory function — Available directly in the script context. Create additional authenticated Octokit clients with different tokens for multi-token workflows, GitHub App tokens, and cross-org access. See Creating additional clients with getOctokit for details and examples.
  • Orchestration ID in user-agent — The ACTIONS_ORCHESTRATION_ID environment variable is automatically appended to the user-agent string for request tracing.

Breaking changes:

  • require('@actions/github') no longer works in scripts. The upgrade to @actions/github v9 (ESM-only) means require('@actions/github') will fail at runtime. If you previously used patterns like const { getOctokit } = require('@actions/github') to create secondary clients, use the new injected getOctokit function instead — it's available directly in the script context with no imports needed.
  • getOctokit is now an injected function parameter. Scripts that declare const getOctokit = ... or let getOctokit = ... will get a SyntaxError because JavaScript does not allow const/let redeclaratio

Fleet review of PR #529: "build(deps): bump actions/github-script from 7 to 9" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/github-script-9 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/github-script](https://github.com/actions/github-script) from 7 to 9.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/github-script's releases.

v9.0.0

New features:

  • getOctokit factory function — Available directly in the script context. Create additional authenticated Octokit clients with different tokens for multi-token workflows, GitHub App tokens, and cross-org access. See Creating additional clients with getOctokit for details and examples.
  • Orchestration ID in user-agent — The ACTIONS_ORCHESTRATION_ID environment variable is automatically appended to the user-agent string for request tracing.

Breaking changes:

  • require('@actions/github') no longer works in scripts. The upgrade to @actions/github v9 (ESM-only) means require('@actions/github') will fail at runtime. If you previously used patterns like const { getOctokit } = require('@actions/github') to create secondary clients, use the new injected getOctokit function instead — it's available directly in the script context with no imports needed.
  • getOctokit is now an injected function parameter. Scripts that declare const getOctokit = ... or let getOctokit = ... will get a SyntaxError because JavaScript does not allow const/let redeclaratio

Fleet review of PR #531: "build(deps): bump actions/upload-artifact from 4 to 7" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/upload-artifact-7 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/upload-artifact](https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact) from 4 to 7.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754
  • Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#762
  • Support direct file uploads by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#764

Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754
  • Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#762
  • Support direct file uploads by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#764

Fleet review of PR #529: "build(deps): bump actions/github-script from 7 to 9" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/github-script-9 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/github-script](https://github.com/actions/github-script) from 7 to 9.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/github-script's releases.

v9.0.0

New features:

  • getOctokit factory function — Available directly in the script context. Create additional authenticated Octokit clients with different tokens for multi-token workflows, GitHub App tokens, and cross-org access. See Creating additional clients with getOctokit for details and examples.
  • Orchestration ID in user-agent — The ACTIONS_ORCHESTRATION_ID environment variable is automatically appended to the user-agent string for request tracing.

Breaking changes:

  • require('@actions/github') no longer works in scripts. The upgrade to @actions/github v9 (ESM-only) means require('@actions/github') will fail at runtime. If you previously used patterns like const { getOctokit } = require('@actions/github') to create secondary clients, use the new injected getOctokit function instead — it's available directly in the script context with no imports needed.
  • getOctokit is now an injected function parameter. Scripts that declare const getOctokit = ... or let getOctokit = ... will get a SyntaxError because JavaScript does not allow const/let redeclaratio

Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

Release notes

Sourced from actions/github-script's releases.

v9.0.0

New features:

  • getOctokit factory function — Available directly in the script context. Create additional authenticated Octokit clients with different tokens for multi-token workflows, GitHub App tokens, and cross-org access. See Creating additional clients with getOctokit for details and examples.
  • Orchestration ID in user-agent — The ACTIONS_ORCHESTRATION_ID environment variable is automatically appended to the user-agent string for request tracing.

Breaking changes:

  • require('@actions/github') no longer works in scripts. The upgrade to @actions/github v9 (ESM-only) means require('@actions/github') will fail at runtime. If you previously used patterns like const { getOctokit } = require('@actions/github') to create secondary clients, use the new injected getOctokit function instead — it's available directly in the script context with no imports needed.
  • getOctokit is now an injected function parameter. Scripts that declare const getOctokit = ... or let getOctokit = ... will get a SyntaxError because JavaScript does not allow const/let redeclaratio

  • getOctokit factory function — Available directly in the script context. Create additional authenticated Octokit clients with different tokens for multi-token workflows, GitHub App tokens, and cross-org access. See Creating additional clients with getOctokit for details and examples.
  • Orchestration ID in user-agent — The ACTIONS_ORCHESTRATION_ID environment variable is automatically appended to the user-agent string for request tracing.

Breaking changes:

  • require('@actions/github') no longer works in scripts. The upgrade to @actions/github v9 (ESM-only) means require('@actions/github') will fail at runtime. If you previously used patterns like const { getOctokit } = require('@actions/github') to create secondary clients, use the new injected getOctokit function instead — it's available directly in the script context with no imports needed.
  • getOctokit is now an injected function parameter. Scripts that declare const getOctokit = ... or let getOctokit = ... will get a SyntaxError because JavaScript does not allow const/let redeclaratio

Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754
  • Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#762
  • Support direct file uploads by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#764

New Contributors

Fleet review of PR #529: "build(deps): bump actions/github-script from 7 to 9" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/github-script-9 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/github-script](https://github.com/actions/github-script) from 7 to 9.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/github-script's releases.

v9.0.0

New features:

  • getOctokit factory function — Available directly in the script context. Create additional authenticated Octokit clients with different tokens for multi-token workflows, GitHub App tokens, and cross-org access. See Creating additional clients with getOctokit for details and examples.
  • Orchestration ID in user-agent — The ACTIONS_ORCHESTRATION_ID environment variable is automatically appended to the user-agent string for request tracing.

Breaking changes:

  • require('@actions/github') no longer works in scripts. The upgrade to @actions/github v9 (ESM-only) means require('@actions/github') will fail at runtime. If you previously used patterns like const { getOctokit } = require('@actions/github') to create secondary clients, use the new injected getOctokit function instead — it's available directly in the script context with no imports needed.
  • getOctokit is now an injected function parameter. Scripts that declare const getOctokit = ... or let getOctokit = ... will get a SyntaxError because JavaScript does not allow const/let redeclaratio

Fleet review of PR #535: "build(deps-dev): bump @playwright/test from 1.60.0 to 1.61.0" Branch: dependabot/npm_and_yarn/playwright/test-1.61.0 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, javascript ## PR Description Bumps [@playwright/test](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright) from 1.60.0 to 1.61.0.
Release notes

Sourced from @​playwright/test's releases.

v1.61.0

🔑 WebAuthn passkeys

New Credentials virtual authenticator, available via browserContext.credentials, lets tests register passkeys and answer navigator.credentials.create() / navigator.credentials.get() ceremonies in the page — no real hardware key required, works in all browsers:

const context = await browser.newContext();

// Seed a passkey your backend provisioned for a test user.

await context.credentials.create('example.com', {

id: credentialId,

userHandle,

privateKey,

publicKey,

});

await context.credentials.install();


const page = await context.newPage();

await page.goto('https://example.com/login');

// The page's navigator.credentials.get() is answered with the seeded passkey.

You can also let the app register a passkey once in a setup test, read it back with credentials.get(), and seed it into later tests — see Credentials for details.

New WebStorage API, available via

Fleet review of PR #531: "build(deps): bump actions/upload-artifact from 4 to 7" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/upload-artifact-7 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/upload-artifact](https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact) from 4 to 7.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754
  • Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#762
  • Support direct file uploads by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#764

Fleet review of PR #529: "build(deps): bump actions/github-script from 7 to 9" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/github-script-9 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/github-script](https://github.com/actions/github-script) from 7 to 9.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/github-script's releases.

v9.0.0

New features:

  • getOctokit factory function — Available directly in the script context. Create additional authenticated Octokit clients with different tokens for multi-token workflows, GitHub App tokens, and cross-org access. See Creating additional clients with getOctokit for details and examples.
  • Orchestration ID in user-agent — The ACTIONS_ORCHESTRATION_ID environment variable is automatically appended to the user-agent string for request tracing.

Breaking changes:

  • require('@actions/github') no longer works in scripts. The upgrade to @actions/github v9 (ESM-only) means require('@actions/github') will fail at runtime. If you previously used patterns like const { getOctokit } = require('@actions/github') to create secondary clients, use the new injected getOctokit function instead — it's available directly in the script context with no imports needed.
  • getOctokit is now an injected function parameter. Scripts that declare const getOctokit = ... or let getOctokit = ... will get a SyntaxError because JavaScript does not allow const/let redeclaratio

Release notes

Sourced from @​playwright/test's releases.

v1.61.0

🔑 WebAuthn passkeys

New Credentials virtual authenticator, available via browserContext.credentials, lets tests register passkeys and answer navigator.credentials.create() / navigator.credentials.get() ceremonies in the page — no real hardware key required, works in all browsers:

const context = await browser.newContext();

// Seed a passkey your backend provisioned for a test user.

await context.credentials.create('example.com', {

id: credentialId,

userHandle,

privateKey,

publicKey,

});

await context.credentials.install();


const page = await context.newPage();

await page.goto('https://example.com/login');

// The page's navigator.credentials.get() is answered with the seeded passkey.

You can also let the app register a passkey once in a setup test, read it back with credentials.get(), and seed it into later tests — see Credentials for details.

Fleet review of PR #531: "build(deps): bump actions/upload-artifact from 4 to 7" Branch: dependabot/github_actions/actions/upload-artifact-7 → master | Author: app/dependabot | Labels: dependencies, github_actions ## PR Description Bumps [actions/upload-artifact](https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact) from 4 to 7.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754

## PR Description
## Summary

Closes two Σ₀ gap-analysis items from #509, in the claude lane.

### #504 — make SIGMA0-COLLAPSE-CERTIFICATE §6 reproducible
The §6 router demonstration referenced two scripts that were **absent from every branch**, so the "parrot attractor" numbers couldn't be verified. Implemented Path A (make it real):

- **`experiments/router_sigma0_encoder.py`** — encodes conversation logs into `[novelty, self_repeat, echo, length]` state vectors and fits sliding-window Jacobians via finite difference.
- **`experiments/router_reservoir_G.py`** — echo-state reservoir (size 50, spectral radius 0.9) with a ridge readout; autonomous rollout projects back onto `[0,1]⁴` (= π).
- **`data/sigma0/{router-encoder,reservoir-G}-output.jsonl`** — checked-in run logs (real, 2678-turn conversation corpus).
- Rewrote §6, Appendix A, and the §7 caveat with the **produced** numbers.

**Reproduced results (real run, 2678 turns):**
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Mean Jacobian spectral radius | 1.064 |
| Fraction of windows ρ > 1 | 0.346 |
| Reservoir held-out MSE | 0.0097 |
| Correlation dimension | 0.74 |
| Autonomous fixed point | novelty 0.78, self_repeat 0.02, echo 0.25, length 0.12 |

**Honesty note:** the real data does **not** collapse to the hand-entered "parrot attractor" (novelty≈1, echo≈0.72). It settles at high-novelty / low-echo instead. The qualitative Σ₀ claim (ungrounded recursion → degenerate low-dim fixed point) holds; the earlier specific numbers were not data-derived and have been replaced.

### #506 — couple surprise monitor to Σ₀ proximity + anti-collapse operator
`SurpriseMonitor` (`src/cio_sde/surprise.py`) now:
- tracks a rolling NIS window
- `sigma0_proximity()` → `{proximity, mean_nis, window_size, alert}` (proximity = clip((mean_nis − baseline)/(threshold

Fleet review of PR #556: "feat(creator): TaskProgressPanel — stage-aware progress with ETA, live stats, and completion summary"
Branch: feature/safezone-facecam-v10 → master | Author: Mookman11 | Labels: none

## PR Description
## Summary

- **Replaces the frozen single progress bar** with a professional multi-stage progress system (inspired by YouTube Studio / Adobe Premiere export UX)
- Adds `stages[]`, `logs[]`, `liveStats{}`, and `etaSeconds` to the Job model so every operation reports meaningful real-time state
- Provides a reusable `TaskProgressPanel` frontend module used by Analyze, Safe Zones, and Render operations

## What changed

### Backend (`job-queue.js`, `job-worker.js`)
- `Job` class gains `stages`, `logs`, `liveStats`, `etaSeconds`, `currentStageId` — all serialised in `toJSON()` and returned by `/api/creator/job/:id`
- `STAGE_MANIFESTS` defines named stages + weights for all 4 job types (`analyze`, `export`, `safezones`, `caption`)
- `executeJob` builds a `ctx` object (`ctx.progress`, `ctx.stage`, `ctx.log`, `ctx.liveStats`) and initialises stages at job start; ETA is recomputed on every progress tick from elapsed time vs. reported percentage
- All 4 job handlers converted to use `ctx` — no changes to scoring, rendering, or detection logic
- Highlight engine `statusKey` values (`loading_video`, `analyzing_motion`, `detecting_highlights`) drive stage transitions without modifying the engine
- Motion progress messages parsed for live `analyzedSec` / `totalSec` stats

### Frontend (`entry.html`)
- New `TPP` IIFE module: `TPP.show(opName)`, `TPP.update(job)`, `TPP.complete(job, summary)`, `TPP.fail(job, stageName)`, `TPP.hide()`
- Smooth progress interpolation via 80 ms interval (`diff * 0.12` easing — never stalls)
- ETA countdown ticks every second between polls
- Stage list with ✓ / ⟳ / ○ / ✗ per-stage icons
- Live stats strip (scanned time, highlights found, top score, regions)
- Expandable analysis log with timestamps (capped at 200 entries)
- Com

## Root Causes Addressed

### 1. Progress bar frozen at 10% (Critical UX Bug)

**Root cause:** `analyzeVideoForHighlights` fires only 4-5 progress callbacks during a full analysis (at ~8%, ~12%, ~50%, ~66%). The dashboard polled these raw values directly and displayed them with no interpolation, so the bar visually sat frozen for 2-5 minutes at each step.

**Fix:** TaskProgressPanel adds:
- Smooth interpolation: `_smoothPct += diff * 0.

**Root cause:** `analyzeVideoForHighlights` fires only 4-5 progress callbacks during a full analysis (at ~8%, ~12%, ~50%, ~66%). The dashboard polled these raw values directly and displayed them with no interpolation, so the bar visually sat frozen for 2-5 minutes at each step.

**Fix:** TaskProgressPanel adds:
- Smooth interpolation: `_smoothPct += diff * 0.

## PR Description
## Summary

Bundles the post-#554 claude-lane work plus the revived content from the closed-dirty PR #557 (Mookman11's sigma0 maintenance cycle), merged cleanly and re-verified.

### Dream Chat provider routing + token cost
- **Auto-mode honors the Σ₀ router-gate's Claude escalation** (`stream-chat.js`): `primaryProviderHint` was computed and discarded; now `autoPrefersAnthropic` skips Ollama/Gemini first-attempts when the gate escalates a substantive turn to the reasoning chain. Explicit picks and non-escalated Auto turns unchanged. Verified in dev preview.
- **Prompt caching on the Claude system block** (`stream-chat.js`, `dream-chat.js`): `cache_control:{type:ephemeral}` on the stable system prefix. Verified against the API (haiku-4.5): a ~7.6k-token prefix wrote then read 7042 cache tokens (~90% saving); small prefixes are a silent no-op below the 4096-token minimum.
- **Audit:** `AUTO_RESOLVE_PROVIDER_AUDIT.md` — documents that Auto was never hardcoded to OpenAI (OpenAI is last in both chains); the real cause of "Claude never answers" was an empty `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`.
- `anti-sprawl.yml`: add the established `experiments/` dir to the allow-list (false-positive block).

### Revived from PR #557
- **Job instrumentation** — `lib/job-queue.js`, `lib/job-worker.js`
- **Creator dashboard** — `public/entry.html`, `routes/creator.js`
- **Audit docs** — `docs/{button-audit,creator-dashboard-audit,sigma0-issue-audit}.md`
- **Maintenance script** — `scripts/sigma0.js`
- **E2E tests** — `tests/e2e/creator-dashboard.spec.js`

## Test plan
- [x] `node --check` on all changed JS (job-queue/worker, creator, sigma0.js, stream/dream-chat, server)
- [x] Python compiles; sigma0 encoder still runs
- [x] Server boots clean (no new errors)
- [x] Dream Chat: explicit Claude

- **Auto-mode honors the Σ₀ router-gate's Claude escalation** (`stream-chat.js`): `primaryProviderHint` was computed and discarded; now `autoPrefersAnthropic` skips Ollama/Gemini first-attempts when the gate escalates a substantive turn to the reasoning chain. Explicit picks and non-escalated Auto turns unchanged. Verified in dev preview.
- **Prompt caching on the Claude system block** (`stream-chat.js`, `dream-chat.js`): `cache_control:{type:ephemeral}` on the stable system prefix. Verified against the API (haiku-4.5): a ~7.6k-token prefix wrote then read 7042 cache tokens (~90% saving); small prefixes are a silent no-op below the 4096-token minimum.
- **Audit:** `AUTO_RESOLVE_PROVIDER_AUDIT.md` — documents that Auto was never hardcoded to OpenAI (OpenAI is last in both chains); the real cause of "Claude never answers" was an empty `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`.
- `anti-sprawl.yml`: add the established `experiments/` dir to the allow-list (false-positive block).

- **Job instrumentation** — `lib/job-queue.js`, `lib/job-worker.js`
